taxonomy: Chenolea biflora is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Rutaceae. It belongs to the genus Chenolea, which consists of a small number of species. The classification of Chenolea biflora is further supported by its morphological characteristics and geographical distribution. Chenolea biflora is native to Australia, where it grows in a variety of habitats. As a member of the Rutaceae family, Chenolea biflora is closely related to other citrus and related plants.
uses: Chenolea biflora, a plant native to the Pacific Islands, has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. The plant's leaves and flowers are said to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making them useful in treating skin conditions and wounds.
